messages.content: My POD doesn’t include the FROL time blocks.

Symptom: Lauren's POD (Plan of the Day) is missing the Family Rule of Life time blocks that should be automatically populated from her daily schedule template.

Likely cause: The issue is most likely in daily_schedule_engine.py in the build_schedule() function or the CHILDREN["Lauren"] configuration block, where the Rule of Life time slots should be processed and converted into POD entries but aren't being properly integrated into her daily plan structure.

Risk areas: Changes to daily_schedule_engine.py could affect POD generation for all family members, disrupt the carryover logic that moves incomplete tasks between days, or break the school sub-item expansion that creates detailed lesson breakdowns for the boys.

Replit prompt:
Fix the Family Rule of Life integration in daily_schedule_engine.py so that Lauren's POD properly includes her FROL time blocks. The build_schedule() function should be processing Lauren's daily schedule template from the family_schedule.json file and converting those time slots into proper POD entries that appear in her /today view. Check that the CHILDREN["Lauren"] configuration properly loads and processes her FROL template, and ensure the time blocks are being added to her schedule structure with the correct formatting. Follow Python 3.11 constraints: no backslashes inside f-strings and keep all imports at the top of the file. Do not change any file other than daily_schedule_engine.py unless absolutely necessary. Preserve all existing elif routing structure in do_POST and do_GET — do not convert any elif chain into separate if blocks and do not reorder existing elif branches. After making changes, confirm nothing in do_POST or do_GET was restructured.
